message:
test(fuzz): seed DAG and bipartite corpora with libfuzzer discoveries

Commit the libfuzzer-discovered seed inputs for the new
fuzz_dag and fuzz_bipartite targets introduced earlier in this push,
so the corpus is reproducible and portable like the rest of
fuzz/corpus/* (fuzz_adjlist, fuzz_attribute_value, fuzz_centrality,
fuzz_clustering, fuzz_connectivity, fuzz_edgelist, ...).

- fuzz/corpus/fuzz_dag/ (~40 KB): seeds covering the DagInput
  variant surface — TopologicalSort, ancestors/descendants,
  LongestPath, Antichains (with the 100-iteration guard exercised),
  TransitiveClosure with None reflexive argument, and
  TransitiveReduction. A few inputs exercise non-DAG rejection so
  the harness explores both Ok and Err branches.

- fuzz/corpus/fuzz_bipartite/ (~29 KB): seeds for the
  BipartiteInput surface — IsBipartiteArbitrary,
  IsBipartiteKnown (the struct-result path fixed up in the
  align-targets commit), and BipartiteSets{Arbitrary,Known}.

No code changes. These are the libfuzzer seed files only, stored as
raw bytes so `cargo fuzz run fuzz_dag -- -corpus=fuzz/corpus/fuzz_dag
...` replays the same exploration cheaply on a fresh worker.
